title: Cluster Networking, CNI, NetworkPolicy, Dual Stack, and Layered Troubleshooting · Kubernetes Retrieval
summary: An on-call handoff reconstructs cluster networking, cni, networkpolicy, dual stack, and layered troubleshooting from delayed object status, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the network intent and isolation evidence policy under changed capacity. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for network intent and isolation evidence policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence de…
objectives: Trace Pod addressing, routing, CNI responsibilities, Service implementation, node paths, NAT, MTU, and platform-specific exceptions.
Interpret single or dual-stack families, family policy, ClusterIPs, EndpointSlices, service proxies, source addresses, and asymmetric reachability.
Derive ingress and egress isolation from Pod and namespace selectors, ipBlock, ports, peers, policy types, and additive allow rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Network Layered Diagnosis
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Pod can resolve DNS but cannot reach a backend after default deny; the policy selects only ingress, misses egress DNS, and assumes NetworkPolicy implementation behavior. Trace source, destination, port, direction, selector, CNI, and node boundary. Competency probe: Diagnose name, endpoint, bind, policy, route, proxy, gateway, certificate, and application failures with source and destination evidence.

Inspect the K8s Network Layered Diagnosis trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Network Layered Diagnosis.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for diagnose name, endpoint, bind, policy, route, proxy, gateway, certificate, and application failures with source and destination evidence. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, keep K8s Network Layered Diagnosis connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: For K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Network Layered Diagnosis precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A review Pod can resolve DNS but cannot reach a backend after default deny; the policy selects only ingress, misses egress DNS, and assumes NetworkPolicy implementation behavior. Trace source, destination, port, direction, selector, CNI, and node boundary. Competency probe: Diagnose name, endpoint, bind, policy, route, proxy, gateway, certificate, and application failures with source and destination evidence.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Successful DNS resolution proves the Service has reachable ready backends.

stimulus.title: K8s Network Layered Diagnosis case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review Pod can resolve DNS but cannot reach a backend after default deny; the policy selects only ingress, misses egress DNS, and assumes NetworkPolicy implementation behavior. Trace source, destination, port, direction, selector, CNI, and node boundary. Competency probe: Diagnose name, endpoint, bind, policy, route, proxy, gateway, certificate, and application failures with source and destination evidence.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Successful DNS resolution proves the Service has reachable ready backends.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose name, endpoint, bind, policy, route, proxy, gateway, certificate, and application failures with source and destination evidence. Artifact under review: network intent and isolation evidence policy.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.
